title: Understanding Amazon Seller Fees in 2026
excerpt: Referral fees, FBA fulfillment, storage, and the extras that quietly erode margin. A clear breakdown of what selling on Amazon actually costs.

blocks.text: Amazon's fees aren't a single number — they're a stack. Two sellers quoting “15%” can have wildly different real costs once fulfillment, storage, and extras are added. Understanding the full stack is the difference between a product that's profitable on paper and one that actually is.

blocks.caption: Amazon US seller fees (headline rates, verified May 2026 — confirm current rates in Seller Central)

cells: Most categories; up to 17% apparel, 45% device accessories

blocks.title: Referral fee includes payment processing
blocks.text: Unlike platforms that itemize a separate payment-processing fee, Amazon bundles it into the referral fee. That makes a flat comparison with other marketplaces misleading — always compare the full stack, not the headline rate.
The fees that sneak up on you

items: Long-term storage fees on inventory that isn’t selling fast enough
Removal and disposal fees when you clear out aged stock
Returns processing fees on fulfilled orders
Advertising (PPC) spend — optional, but often necessary to compete
Aged-inventory surcharges during Q4 when storage costs spike

blocks.title: Q4 storage is a margin trap
blocks.text: Storage fees rise sharply in the October–December peak. Overstock going into Q4 “just in case” can quietly turn a profitable SKU into a loss. Forecast tightly.
How to actually price a product

items.title: Start from the full fee stack
items.text: Add referral + fulfillment + an allocation for storage and returns — not just the referral percentage.

items.title: Add your landed cost
items.text: Include manufacturing, freight, duties, and inbound shipping to Amazon.

items.title: Account for advertising
items.text: For competitive categories, budget PPC as a real cost of doing business, not an afterthought.

items.title: Check the margin that’s left
items.text: If the remaining margin is thin before you’ve spent a dollar on ads, the product may not work on Amazon.

blocks.text: Research tools with built-in profit calculators make this faster — see our reviews to compare them. And don't forget the other side of the ledger: our reimbursement guide covers recovering fees and inventory Amazon owes you back.
